title: Solve area with the distributive property
description: Solve area with the distributive property.
topic_path: Topic C: Arithmetic properties using area models
Solve area with the distributive property

assessment_items.question_content:
Each square in the figure below has an area of $1$ square unit. We can break the figure into two parts. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the equations to find the area of the entire figure.**  

||
-: | :-
$\text{Area}$ |$=$  [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\redE{\text{ fives}} + \purpleE{4\text{ fives}}$|
|$= \redE{10} + \purpleE{20}$|
|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]]|
hints:
The rectangle has an area of $6\times 5$, or  $6$ fives.

Let's use the figure to break the $6$ fives into two parts.

The striped rectangle breaks up $6$ fives into $\redE{2\text{ fives}}$ and $\purpleE{4\text{ fives}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The top rectangle has an area of $\redE{2\text{ fives}}$ or $\redE{10}$.

The bottom rectangle has an area of $\purpleE{4\text{ fives}}$ or $\purpleE{20}$.

$\begin{align}
6\times 5&=\redE{2\text{ fives}} + \purpleE{4\text{ fives}}\\\\
&= \redE{10} + \purpleE{20}\\\\
&= 30
\end{align}$

options.alt: A rectangle divided into unit squares with 6 rows and 5 columns. A curved bracket across the rows is labeled 6 and a curved bracket across the columns is labeled 5. The first 2 rows are shaded red and a curved bracket across these rows is labeled 2. The last 4 rows are shaded purple and a curved bracket across these rows is labeled 4.

assessment_items.question_content:

-: | :-
$\text{Area}$ |$= \redE{3\text{ nines}} + \purpleE{3\text{ nines}}$|
|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $ {}+ \purpleE{27}$|
|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]]|
hints:
The rectangle has an area of $6\times 9$, or  $6$ nines.

Let's use the figure to break the $6$ nines into two parts.

The striped rectangle breaks up $6$ nines into $\redE{3\text{ nines}}$ and $\purpleE{3\text{ nines}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The top rectangle has an area of $\redE{3\text{ nines}}$ or $\redE{27}$.

The bottom rectangle has an area of $\purpleE{3\text{ nines}}$ or $\purpleE{27}$.

$\begin{align}
9\times 6&=\redE{3\text{ nines}} + \purpleE{3\text{ nines}}\\\\
&=\redE{27} + \purpleE{27}\\\\
&= 54
\end{align}$

options.alt: A rectangle divided into unit squares with 6 rows and 9 columns. A curved bracket across the rows is labeled 6 and a curved bracket across the columns is labeled 9. The first 3 rows are shaded red and a curved bracket across these rows is labeled 3. The last 3 rows are shaded purple and a curved bracket across these rows is labeled 3.

-: | :-
$\text{Area}$ |$=\purpleD{5}$ [[☃ dropdown 1]]$+ \maroonE{2\text{ fours}}$|
|$= \purpleD{20} +\maroonE{8}$|
|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]]|
hints:
The rectangle has an area of $7\times4$, or  $7$ fours.

Let's use the figure to break the $7$ fours into two parts.

The striped rectangle breaks up $7$ fours into $\purpleD{5\text{ fours}}$ and $\maroonE{2\text{ fours}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The top rectangle has an area of $\purpleD{5\text{ fours}}$ or $\purpleD{20}$.

The bottom rectangle has an area of $\maroonE{2\text{ fours}}$ or $\maroonE{8}$.

$\begin{align}
7\times 4&=\purpleD{5\text{ fours}} + \maroonE{2\text{ fours}}\\\\
&= \purpleD{20} + \maroonE{8}\\\\
&= 28
\end{align}$

options.alt: A rectangle divided into unit squares with 7 rows and 4 columns. A curved bracket across the rows is labeled 7 and a curved bracket across the columns is labeled 4. The first 5 rows are shaded purple and a curved bracket across these rows is labeled 5. The last 2 rows are shaded maroon and a curved bracket across these rows is labeled 2.

hints:
The rectangle has an area of $6\times5$, or  $6$ fives.

Let's use the figure to break the $6$ fives into two parts.

The striped rectangle breaks up $6$ fives into $\purpleE{3\text{ fives}}$ and $\maroonD{3\text{ fives}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The top rectangle has an area of $\purpleE{3\text{ fives}}$ or $\purpleE{15}$.

The bottom rectangle has an area of $\maroonD{3\text{ fives}}$ or $\maroonD{15}$.

$\begin{align}
6\times 5&=\purpleE{3\text{ fives}} + \maroonD{3\text{ fives}}\\\\
&= \purpleE{15} + \maroonD{15}\\\\
&= 30
\end{align}$

|$= \purpleE{14}{} +{} $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]|
|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]|
hints:
The rectangle has an area of $7\times7$, or  $7$ sevens.

Let's use the figure to break the $7$ sevens into two parts.

The striped rectangle breaks up $7$ sevens into $\purpleE{2\text{ sevens}}$ and $\maroonD{5\text{ sevens}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The top rectangle has an area of $\purpleE{2\text{ sevens}}$ or $\purpleE{14}$.

The bottom rectangle has an area of $\maroonD{5\text{ sevens}}$ or $\maroonD{35}$.

$\begin{align}
7\times 7=&~\purpleE{2\text{ sevens}}+ \maroonD{5\text{ sevens}}\\\\
=&~ \purpleE{14} + \maroonD{35}\\\\
=&~ 49
\end{align}$

|$= \goldD{30}{} +{} $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]|
|$={}$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]|
hints:
The rectangle has an area of $6\times7$, or  $6$ sevens.

Let's use the figure to break the $6$ sevens into two parts.

The striped rectangle breaks up $6$ sevens into $\goldD{6\text{ fives}}$ and $\blueD{6\text{ twos}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The top rectangle has an area of $\goldD{6\text{ fives}}$ or $\goldD{30}$.

The bottom rectangle has an area of $\blueD{6\text{ twos}}$ or $\blueD{12}$.

$\begin{align}
6\times 7=&~\goldD{6\text{ fives}}+ \blueE{6\text{ twos}}\\\\
=&~ \goldD{30} + \blueD{12}\\\\
=&~ 42
\end{align}$

options.alt: A square divided into unit squares with 7 rows and 6 columns. A curved bracket across the rows is labeled 7 and a curved bracket across the columns is labeled 6. ||
-: | :-
$\text{Area}$ |$= \redE{4\text{ fours}} +{}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\purpleE{\text{ fours}}$|
|$=\redE{16}+\purpleE{20}$ |
|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]]|
hints:
The rectangle has an area of $9\times 4$, or  $9$ fours.

Let's use the figure to break the $9$ fours into two parts.

The striped rectangle breaks up $9$ fours into $\redE{4\text{ fours}}$ and $\purpleE{5\text{ fours}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The left rectangle has an area of $\redE{4\text{ fours}}$ or $\redE{16}$.

The right rectangle has an area of $\purpleE{5\text{ fours}}$ or $\purpleE{20}$.

$\begin{align}
9\times 4 &=\redE{4\text{ fours}} + \purpleE{5\text{ fours}}\\\\
&= \redE{16} + \purpleE{20}\\\\
&= 36
\end{align}$

hints:
The rectangle has an area of $8\times 6$, or  $8$ sixes.

Let's use the figure to break the $8$ sixes into two parts.

The striped rectangle breaks up $8$ sixes into $\purpleE{3\text{ sixes}}$ and $\maroonE{5\text{ sixes}}$.

The left rectangle has an area of $\purpleE{3\text{ sixes}}$ or $\purpleE{18}$.

The right rectangle has an area of $\maroonE{5\text{ sixes}}$ or $\maroonE{30}$.

$\begin{align}
8\times 6 &=\purpleE{3\text{ sixes}} + \maroonE{5\text{ sixes}}\\\\
&= \purpleE{18} + \maroonE{30}\\\\
&= 48
\end{align}$

Let's use the figure to break the $7$ eights into two parts.

=&~ \purpleE{32} + \redE{24}\\\\
=&~ 56
\end{align}$

|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $ {} + \purpleD{8}$|
|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]]|
hints:
The rectangle has an area of $8\times4$, or  $8$ fours.

Let's use the figure to break the $8$ fours into two parts.

The striped rectangle breaks up $8$ fours into $\redE{6\text{ fours}}$ and $\purpleD{2\text{ fours}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The left rectangle has an area of $\redE{6\text{ fours}}$ or $\redE{24}$.

The right rectangle has an area of $\purpleD{2\text{ fours}}$ or $\purpleD{8}$.

$\begin{align}
8\times 4 &=\redE{6\text{ fours}} + \purpleD{2\text{ fours}}\\\\
&=\redE{24} + \purpleD{8}\\\\
&= 32
\end{align}$

|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $ {} +\maroonE{15}$|
|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]]|
hints:
The rectangle has an area of $8\times5$, or  $8$ fives.

Let's use the figure to break the $8$ fives into two parts.

The striped rectangle breaks up $8$ fives into $\purpleE{5\text{ fives}}$ and $\maroonE{3\text{ fives}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The left rectangle has an area of $\purpleE{5\text{ fives}}$ or $\purpleE{25}$.

The right rectangle has an area of $\maroonE{3\text{ fives}}$ or $\maroonE{15}$.

$\begin{align}
8\times5 &=\purpleE{5\text{ fives}} + \maroonE{3\text{ fives}}\\\\
&= \purpleE{25} + \maroonE{15}\\\\
&= 40
\end{align}$
